summary: On the night of June 28, 2026, Russia launched a large-scale combined missile and drone attack on Ukraine, firing two Oniks/Zircon anti-ship missiles from Kursk Oblast, six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles from Bryansk Oblast, and 142 strike drones from multiple directions. Ukrainian air defenses shot down or suppressed one Oniks/Zircon, six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 125 enemy drones across the north, south, and east of the country, though impacts were recorded at 11 locations. The attack included ballistic missile threats against Kyiv and Kharkiv Oblast, with air raid alerts across multiple northern and eastern regions.
key_points: Russia launched a massive overnight strike on June 28, 2026, using two Oniks/Zircon missiles from Kursk Oblast, six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles from Bryansk Oblast, and 142 strike drones from multiple launch points. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)

Ukrainian air defenses intercepted one Oniks/Zircon, six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 125 enemy drones, though impacts were recorded at 11 locations across the country. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)

Ballistic missile threats targeted Kyiv and Chuhuiv in Kharkiv Oblast, with air raid alerts issued for the capital and multiple northern and eastern regions. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68143, @mon1tor_ua/68137)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs (KAB) at Zaporizhzhia Oblast and Sumy Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66644)
Russian forces struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in the Mykolaiv region with Geranium drones, hitting boat and unmanned craft depots as well as personnel. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222)
The Russian Armed Forces liberated the villages of Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk Oblast and Novoselovka in Zaporizhzhia Oblast. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221)

Multiple drone groups were tracked heading toward Sumy, Shostka, Kharkiv, Poltava, Kremenchuk, and other cities across northern and eastern Ukraine throughout the day. (Source: @kpszsu/66611, @kpszsu/66638, @kpszsu/66642)
A mobilization incident in Lutsk showed a man being pushed into a van while city residents attempted unsuccessfully to free him. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31220)

sections.executive_summary: On the night of June 28, 2026, Russia conducted a large-scale combined missile and drone attack on Ukraine, firing Oniks/Zircon anti-ship missiles from Kursk Oblast, Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles from Bryansk Oblast, and 142 strike drones from multiple directions including Kursk, Oryol, Millerovo, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, and occupied Donetsk and Crimea. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted the majority of incoming threats, shooting down one Oniks/Zircon, six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 125 drones, though impacts were confirmed at 11 locations. The attack represents a significant escalation in Russia's long-range strike campaign, with ballistic missiles specifically targeting Kyiv and Kharkiv Oblast alongside sustained drone pressure across northern, eastern, and southern Ukraine.

points: Ukrainian air defense forces shot down or suppressed one Oniks/Zircon anti-ship missile, six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 125 enemy drones during the overnight attack of June 28, 2026, with aviation, anti-aircraft missile troops, electronic warfare units, mobile fire groups, and unmanned systems units all participating. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)
Despite successful interceptions, missile strikes and 14 strike drones hit 11 locations across Ukraine, with falling debris also recorded in some areas. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)

Air raid alerts were issued for Kyiv at 05:10 on June 28, with ballistic missile threats specifically targeting the capital and Chuhuiv in Kharkiv Oblast, and high alerts for further Iskander-M launches from Bryansk Oblast. (Source: @eRadarrua/98468, @mon1tor_ua/68143, @mon1tor_ua/68137, @mon1tor_ua/68145)

A Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in the Mykolaiv region was struck by Russian Geranium drones, with boat and unmanned craft depots and personnel hit; the Russian Ministry of Defense released objective monitoring footage. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222)
A mobilization incident in Lutsk showed a man being pushed into a van while city residents attempted to free him but were unsuccessful. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31220)

Multiple drone groups were tracked across Sumy, Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Poltava, Dnipropetrovsk, and Mykolaiv oblasts throughout the day, with some heading toward Sumy, Shostka, Kharkiv, Poltava, Kremenchuk, and Voznesensk. (Source: @kpszsu/66611, @kpszsu/66638, @kpszsu/66642, @kpszsu/66654)

Russia launched a massive overnight strike on June 28, 2026, using two Oniks/Zircon anti-ship missiles from Kursk Oblast, six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles from Bryansk Oblast, and 142 strike drones including Shaheds (including jet-powered variants), Gerbers, Italmas, and Parody-type decoy drones from Kursk, Oryol, Millerovo, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, occupied Donetsk, and Guardske in occupied Crimea. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs (KAB) at targets in Zaporizhzhia Oblast and Sumy Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66644)

The Russian Armed Forces struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in the Mykolaiv region with Geranium drones, hitting depots of boats and unmanned craft as well as personnel. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222)

The Russian Armed Forces liberated the villages of Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk Oblast and Novoselovka in Zaporizhzhia Oblast, according to the Russian Ministry of Defense. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221)

A report from Two Majors indicates the Russian Armed Forces are completing the months-long assault on Konstantinovka in the DPR, isolating and encircling Krasny Lyman, and leveling the front on the Slavyansk direction, with a concentration of forces visible for the upcoming battle for the Slavyansk-Kramatorsk agglomeration. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31218)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs (KAB) at eastern Kharkiv Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66638)
A ballistic missile threat was reported targeting Chuhuiv in Kharkiv Oblast. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68137)

Drone groups were tracked in the Kharkiv suburb area and heading toward Kharkiv from the north, with some remaining in the Shostka and Trostyanets areas of Sumy Oblast. (Source: @eRadarrua/98467, @kpszsu/66611)

The Russian Armed Forces are completing the months-long assault on Konstantinovka in the DPR, isolating and encircling Krasny Lyman, and leveling the front on the Slavyansk direction. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31218)
A concentration of Ukrainian forces is visible for the upcoming battle for the Slavyansk-Kramatorsk agglomeration, from which more and more civilians are leaving. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31218)
The liberation of Donbas from the AFU is being postulated by Moscow as a condition for entering the peace track and as the last unsequestered goal of the SVO. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31218)

Ballistic missile threats targeted Kyiv, with air raid alerts issued at 05:10 on June 28 and high alerts for further Iskander-M launches from Bryansk Oblast. (Source: @eRadarrua/98468, @mon1tor_ua/68143, @mon1tor_ua/68145)

A jet-powered Shahed was tracked heading toward Darnytskyi district of Kyiv, with course changes noted toward Vasylkiv/Obukhiv, and continuing movement toward Bila Tserkva. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68100, @mon1tor_ua/68098, @mon1tor_ua/68103)

Drone groups were tracked across Chernihiv Oblast heading toward Mena and westward, along the border toward Slavutych, and between Sumy and Chernihiv oblasts heading south. (Source: @kpszsu/66609, @eRadarrua/98474, @kpszsu/66638)
Multiple drones headed toward Sumy from the north and south, and toward Shostka in Sumy Oblast, with some remaining in the Shostka and Trostyanets areas. (Source: @kpszsu/66611, @eRadarrua/98467)
Speed-type air-to-air missiles were reported heading toward Sumy Oblast. (Source: @eRadarrua/98504)
A Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in the Mykolaiv region was struck by Russian Geranium drones, with boat and unmanned craft depots and personnel hit. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222)
Drone groups were tracked in Mykolaiv Oblast heading toward Voznesensk. (Source: @kpszsu/66638)

In Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, drones were tracked near Pokrovsk heading north, and a pair of jet-powered drones headed toward Pyatyhatky and further toward Kremenchuk. (Source: @kpszsu/66654, @eRadarrua/98495)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs (KAB) at Zaporizhzhia Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66644)
The Russian Armed Forces liberated the village of Novoselovka in Zaporizhzhia Oblast. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221)
The Russian Armed Forces also liberated the village of Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk Oblast. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221)

Russia launched 142 strike drones including Shaheds (including jet-powered variants), Gerbers, Italmas, and Parody-type decoy drones from multiple directions during the overnight attack of June 28, 2026. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)
Ukrainian air defenses shot down or suppressed 125 enemy drones across the north, south, and east of the country, with 14 strike drones hitting targets at 11 locations. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)
Two Oniks/Zircon anti-ship missiles were launched from Kursk Oblast, with one intercepted by Ukrainian air defense. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)
Six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles were launched from Bryansk Oblast, all reportedly intercepted by Ukrainian air defense. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs (KAB) at Zaporizhzhia Oblast and Sumy Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66644)

A jet-powered Shahed was tracked heading toward Darnytskyi district of Kyiv, with course changes toward Vasylkiv/Obukhiv and continuing toward Bila Tserkva. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68100, @mon1tor_ua/68103)
Speed-type air-to-air missiles were reported heading toward Sumy Oblast. (Source: @eRadarrua/98504)

key_developments: Russia launched a massive combined overnight strike on June 28, 2026, firing two Oniks/Zircon missiles from Kursk Oblast, six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles from Bryansk Oblast, and 142 strike drones from Kursk, Oryol, Millerovo, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, occupied Donetsk, and occupied Crimea. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted one Oniks/Zircon, six Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 125 drones, though impacts were recorded at 11 locations across the country. (Source: @kpszsu/66637)

Ballistic missile threats targeted Kyiv and Chuhuiv in Kharkiv Oblast, with high alerts for further Iskander-M launches from Bryansk Oblast and air raid alerts for the capital at 05:10. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68143, @mon1tor_ua/68137, @mon1tor_ua/68145)
Russian forces struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in the Mykolaiv region with Geranium drones, hitting boat and unmanned craft depots and personnel. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222)

The Russian Armed Forces liberated Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk Oblast and Novoselovka in Zaporizhzhia Oblast, and are completing the assault on Konstantinovka in the DPR while isolating Krasny Lyman. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221, @ukr_leaks_eng/31218)

Multiple drone groups were tracked across Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, Poltava, Dnipropetrovsk, and Mykolaiv oblasts, with jet-powered drones heading toward Pyatyhatky, Kremenchuk, and Bila Tserkva. (Source: @kpszsu/66611, @kpszsu/66638, @eRadarrua/98495, @mon1tor_ua/68103)

Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs (KAB) at Zaporizhzhia Oblast and Sumy Oblast, while speed-type air-to-air missiles were reported heading toward Sumy Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66644, @eRadarrua/98504)
sections.threat_assessment: The overnight attack of June 28, 2026, represents a significant escalation in Russia's long-range strike campaign, combining ballistic missiles (Iskander-M/S-400 from Bryansk Oblast and Oniks/Zircon from Kursk Oblast) with a mass drone salvo of 142 strike drones from multiple launch points. The targeting of Kyiv with ballistic missiles alongside sustained drone pressure across northern, eastern, and southern Ukraine indicates Moscow is maintaining a multi-axis threat posture. Ukrainian air defense performance was strong, intercepting the majority of ballistic missiles and drones, but the 11 impact locations and 14 successful drone strikes demonstrate that saturation tactics continue to pose a challenge. The Russian Armed Forces' continued ground offensives in the DPR (Konstantinovka, Krasny Lyman) and territorial gains in Dnepropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia oblasts suggest Moscow is pursuing simultaneous pressure on both the frontline and rear areas.
sections.regional_response: The EU countries are reportedly shifting their defense preparations from expensive platforms like tanks and new-generation aircraft toward mass production of cheaper means of destruction, including hundreds of thousands of drones, missiles, and guided bombs, as part of a strategy to achieve superiority in a planned conflict with Russia. Ukraine hopes to receive at least $154 billion from Western allies by 2030, according to Ukrainian Ministry of Finance data. The negotiation track remains stalled, with Lavrov expressing frustration over what he characterized as deception during the Anchorage talks, while the USA has made clear it has definitively chosen a side in the conflict. Moscow has framed the liberation of Donbas from the AFU as a precondition for entering peace talks.
sections.intelligence_notes: The Two Majors report indicates the main phase of the Russian Army's summer offensive campaign is underway, with both sides multiplying strikes on each other's rear infrastructure. Russia has reportedly lifted some self-imposed restrictions on strikes, with increased frequency of attacks on Kyiv and large-scale use of FABs in Zaporozhye. The AFU is conducting an information-combat operation on the Crimean direction, intending to gain a stronger negotiation position through military pressure on the peninsula during summer. A concentration of Ukrainian forces is visible for the upcoming battle for the Slavyansk-Kramatorsk agglomeration, from which civilians are increasingly departing. Experts predict a potential new round of negotiations by autumn, though there are currently no diplomatic conditions for this.
